result:
Done. Here's a summary of what was created:

## Reddit Channel Extension (`extensions/reddit/`)

**12 files created:**

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `package.json` | Package manifest with `@openclaw/reddit`, channel metadata, install config |
| `openclaw.plugin.json` | Plugin manifest declaring `reddit` channel |
| `index.ts` | Plugin entry point, registers channel with runtime |
| `src/runtime.ts` | Module-scoped `PluginRuntime` singleton |
| `src/types.ts` | Reddit-specific types (`RedditAccountConfig`, `RedditInboundMessage`, `RedditSendResult`) |
| `src/config-schema.ts` | Zod schema (single + multi-account) |
| `src/accounts.ts` | Account resolution, `listAccountIds`, `isAccountConfigured` |
| `src/reddit-api/client.ts` | OAuth2 REST client with automatic token refresh, `getMe`, `getUnreadMessages`, `markAsRead`, `postComment`, `sendDirectMessage` |
| `src/reddit-api/probe.ts` | Health probe via `/api/v1/me` |
| `src/reddit-api/send.ts` | `sendRedditReply` (comment) + `sendRedditDm` (private message) |
| `src/reddit-api/monitor.ts` | Polling-based monitor for inbox/mentions with agent pipeline dispatch |
| `src/onboarding.ts` | CLI wizard with env var support (`OPENCLAW_REDDIT_CLIENT_ID/SECRET/REFRESH_TOKEN`) |
| `src/outbound.ts` | Outbound adapter supporting thing ID replies and username DMs |
| `src/channel.ts` | Main `ChannelPlugin<RedditAccountConfig>` with `direct`+`group` capabilities |
| `src/channel.test.ts` | **23 tests** covering accounts, plugin config, status snapshots, probe, outbound targeting, and onboarding |

**Test results:** 23/23 passing, 0 lint warnings, formatting clean.
